prompts: In quantum mechanics, electron transitions occur when electrons move between discrete energy levels within an atom. These energy levels are quantized, meaning electrons can only occupy specific energy states. When an electron absorbs energy, it can jump to a higher energy level, known as excitation. Conversely, when an electron releases energy, it falls to a lower energy level, resulting in emission. These transitions are depicted in an energy level diagram, where each line represents a potential energy state. Electron transitions influence the emission or absorption spectra, providing insights into the atomic structure and the behavior of electrons.
